# Senseair Sunrise

Notes from `PSP11704.pdf` (Product Specification, article `006-0-0007`, document rev. `5`, 12 pages).

## Scope

This document is the short-form product specification for the battery-powered `Senseair Sunrise` module. It is the most useful source for electrical limits, pinout, operating ranges, and headline performance numbers.

## General specifications

| Item | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Article number | `006-0-0007` |
| Operating principle | Non-dispersive infrared (NDIR) |
| Measured gas | Carbon dioxide (`CO2`) |
| Measurement range | `0-10000 ppm` |
| Accuracy, ANSI/ASHRAE 62.1 band | `600-2500 ppm: +/-75 ppm` |
| Accuracy, standard operating band | `400-5000 ppm: +/- (30 ppm + 3% of reading)` |
| Accuracy, extended operating band | `5001-10000 ppm: +/- (10% of reading)` |
| Operating conditions | `0-50 C`, `0-85% RH` non-condensing, see Figure 4 in the PDF |
| Warm-up time | `0 s` |
| Response time `T90%` | `30 s` |
| Power supply | `3.05-5.5 V` |
| Peak current | `<125 mA` |
| Steady-state current during sampling | `99 mA` |
| Average current, typical | `45 uA` |
| Default measurement setting | Continuous mode, `16 s`, `8 samples` |
| Communication | `UART / I2C` |
| Pressure dependence | `1.6% of reading per kPa` deviation from normal pressure |
| RMS noise, filtered | `0.6 ppm @ 400 ppm, 25 C`; `2.5 ppm @ 3000 ppm, 25 C` |
| RMS noise, unfiltered | `6 ppm @ 400 ppm, 25 C`; `17 ppm @ 3000 ppm, 25 C` |
| Maintenance | Maintenance-free |
| Life expectancy | `>15 years` |
| Dimensions | `33.5 x 19.7 x 11.5 mm` |
| Weight | `5 g` |
| Storage conditions | `-40 to 70 C` |

## Important notes from the spec

- The sensor is designed for `400-5000 ppm`; the extended range goes to `10000 ppm`.
- Exposure below `400 ppm` can interfere with the ABC algorithm when ABC is enabled.
- Shipping, rough handling, and assembly can temporarily disturb accuracy; the spec says accuracy can recover after forced recalibration or after up to `3` ABC periods.
- The spec states the module is unprotected against surges and reverse connection.
- Sampling start/stop includes a fast transient current; the vendor points to `TDE7318` for integration details.
- The `45 uA` typical average current assumes default settings and `nRDY` disabled.

## Description and intended use

Senseair Sunrise is a miniature `CO2` sensor module intended for battery-powered applications. The vendor positions it as a host-controlled sensor where the host can choose measurement mode, measurement period, and number of samples to trade off accuracy against power.

## Mechanical and handling notes

- The sample gas diffusion area must not be covered.
- Reduced gas circulation can degrade response time.
- For installation and soldering, the spec points to `ANO4947` (Sunrise and Sunlight Handling Manual).
- The dimensions drawing is referenced separately as drawing `740-00993`.

## Pinout

Top-view pin numbering from the PDF:

| Pin | Symbol | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `GND` | Power | Ground |
| 2 | `VBB` | Power | Sensor supply voltage |
| 3 | `VDDIO` | Power | I/O supply for `TXD/SCL` and `nRDY` |
| 4 | `RxD/SDA` | I/O | UART receive / `I2C` bidirectional data; true open-drain when acting as output |
| 5 | `TxD/SCL` | I/O | UART transmit / `I2C` clock; true open-drain when acting as output; `100 kOhm` internal pull-up to `VDDIO` |
| 6 | `COMSEL` | Input | Communication select at power-up: `HIGH = UART` (default, may float), `LOW = I2C` (tie to `GND`) |
| 7 | `nRDY` | Output | Measurement-ready output; true open-drain, active low; `1 MOhm` internal pull-up to `VDDIO` |
| 8 | `DVCC` | Power output | Internal supply output; not intended to power external systems; leave floating if unused |
| 9 | `EN` | Input | Enable, active high; `>1.2 V` turns sensor on, `<0.4 V` enters shutdown; do not float; tie to `VBB` if unused |

## Absolute maximum ratings

Unless otherwise noted, voltages are relative to `GND`.

| Item | Min | Max | Unit |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `VBB`, `VDDIO`, `nRDY`, `RxD/SDA`, `TxD/SCL`, `EN` | `-0.3` | `6` | V |
| `DVCC` | `-0.3` | `VBB + 0.3` or `4.3`, whichever is less | V |
| `COMSEL` with `EN = HIGH` | `-0.3` | `DVCC + 0.3` | V |
| `COMSEL` with `EN = LOW` | `-0.3` | `0.3` | V |
| Instantaneous max current limit on `COMSEL`, `RxD/SDA`, `TxD/SCL` |  | `25` | mA |

The PDF also says the `DVCC` maximum output current is internally limited.

## Recommended operating conditions

| Item | Min | Typ | Max | Unit |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `VBB` supply voltage | `3.05` | `3.3` | `5.5` | V |
| `VDDIO` I/O supply | `0` |  | `5.5` | V |
| `COMSEL` | `0` |  | `DVCC` | V |
| `EN` | `0` |  | `VBB` | V |
| `RxD/SDA` | `0` |  | `VDDIO` | V |
| `TxD/SCL` | `0` |  | `VDDIO` | V |
| `ICOMSEL` DC injection current | `-2` |  | `2` | mA |
| `IDVCC` internal supply current | `0` |  | `25` | mA |

Notes called out in the spec:

- Leave `VDDIO` floating if unused.
- `IDVCC` is limited to the stated value.

## Electrical characteristics

Conditions in the table: `VEN = VBB = 3.3 V`, continuous mode, `16 s` period, `8` samples, unless otherwise noted.

| Item | Condition | Min | Typ | Max | Unit |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `VDVCC` supply output voltage |  | `2.74` |  | `2.91` | V |
| `VIH` input high voltage | `COMSEL`, `RxD/SDA`, `TxD/SCL` | `2.0` |  |  | V |
| `VIH` input high voltage | `ENABLE` | `1.2` |  |  | V |
| `VIL` input low voltage | `COMSEL`, `RxD/SDA`, `TxD/SCL` |  |  | `0.82` | V |
| `VIL` input low voltage | `ENABLE` |  |  | `0.4` | V |
| `VHYS` input hysteresis | `COMSEL`, `RxD/SDA`, `TxD/SCL` |  | `164` |  | mV |
| Operating peak current | `VEN >= 1.2 V`, `3.05 < VBB < 5.5 V` |  |  | `125` | mA |
| Operating average current |  |  | `45` |  | uA |
| Supply quiescent current | `VEN < 0.3 V`, `3.05 < VBB < 5.5 V` | `0.2` |  | `1` | uA |
| `IEN` enable pin leakage current | `VEN = VBB = 5.5 V` |  | `5.5` |  | uA |
| `IVDDIO` I/O supply leakage current | `VDDIO = 3.3 V` | `0.2` |  | `1.1` | uA |
| `IIN` input leakage current | `VDDIO = 3.3 V`, `RxD/SDA`, `TxD/SCL` | `0.1` |  | `1` | uA |

The table notes that `DVCC` is not intended to power external systems, and that the average-current figure assumes `nRDY` is disabled.

## Accuracy envelope from the figures

The accuracy plots on page 8 show three regions:

- ANSI/ASHRAE compliance zone: `15-35 C`, `600-2500 ppm`, `+/-75 ppm`.
- Standard region: `0-50 C`, up to `5000 ppm`, accuracy `+/- (30 ppm + 3% of reading)`.
- Extended region for concentration: `0-50 C`, up to `10000 ppm`, accuracy `+/- (30 ppm + 3% of reading)` or `+/-10% of reading`, whichever is greater.
- Extended region for humidity: `0-50 C`, up to `85% RH` with dew point `<35 C`, using the same greater-of rule.

## Average current and noise figures

The current and noise plots are guidance rather than fixed limits:

- Average current falls as the measurement period increases.
- Average current also falls when the number of samples is reduced.
- The default operating point marked in the figure is the same default listed elsewhere: continuous mode, `16 s`, `8 samples`.
- Measurement RMS noise drops as the sample count increases; the plotted comparison is `1`, `8`, and `32` samples.

## Measurement modes

The spec defines two operating modes:

1. Continuous measurement mode.
   The sensor measures at a fixed interval. The host reads results after each measurement and does not need to trigger each cycle.
2. Single measurement mode.
   The sensor waits for a host command for each measurement.

The default mode is continuous measurement mode. For integration details, the spec explicitly points to `TDE7318` and `TDE5531`.

## Communication references

- UART/Modbus details: `TDE5514`
- `I2C` details: `TDE5531`
- System integration guidance: `TDE7318`
- Handling guidance: `ANO4947`

## Maintenance and calibration stance

The product spec says the module includes a built-in self-correcting ABC algorithm and that the ABC period is host-adjustable. It does not provide the calibration procedure itself; it tells the reader to discuss calibration strategy with Senseair and use the integration manuals for command-level behavior.
